revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23991619004|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:井草八幡宮]] {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Igusa hachimangu entrance.JPG |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Igusa Hachimangu Shrine | image = [[File:Igusa hachimangu entrance.JPG|250px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|57.9|N|139|35|44.1|E}}}} | location = 1-33-1 Zenpukuji, Suginami City, Tokyo, Japan | latitude = 35|longitude = 139 | deity = {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|qid=Q261637|lt=Hachiman|1=Hachiman}} (historically {{ill|Kasuga deity|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}})
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ojin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} (Hachiman Shrine) / {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|qid=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} | established = Heian period (late 8th century - 12th century) | festivals = {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} Evening festival (September 30)
Main festival (October 1)
Events (October 2)
| notable_rituals = {{ill|Shinko-shiki|en|Shinko-shiki|qid=Q123907414|lt=Shinkosai|1=Shinko-shiki}} (every 3 years)
{{ill|Yabusame|en|Yabusame|qid=Q1886157|lt=Yabusame|1=Yabusame}} (every 5 years)
Oume Kaido Procession (other years) }} {{ill|Igusa Hachimangu|ja|井草八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igusa Hachimangu|qq=}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Zenpukuji|ja|善福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpukuji|1=zenpukuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11418965}}, Suginami City, Tokyo, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}{{Cite web |title=experience SUGINAMI |url=https://experience-suginami.tokyo/2016/06/igusa-hachimangu-shrine/#:~:text=Igusa%20Hachimangu%20Shrine%20is%20famous,a%20ritual%20for%20Shogun%20families. |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=experience-suginami.tokyo}}. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}}. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]{{cite web |last=Motegi |first=Sadazumi |title=Shamei Bunpu (Shrine names and Distributions) |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=776 |accessdate=23 March 2010 |publisher=Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=Japanese}}. It is strongly associated with [[Yabusame]]. == Overview == The shrine is located along [[:en:Ōme Kaidō|Ōme Kaidō]] and {{ill|Waseda Dori|ja|早稲田通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waseda Dori|qq=}}. It is renowned for its expansive {{ill|Chinju no Mori|lt=shrine forest|ja|社叢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinju no Mori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守の森}}, one of the largest in Tokyo. Historically, the area was known as Osamuno-i, and the shrine was called "Osamuno-i Hachimangu" until the Meiji period. The shrine has origins tracing back to the {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|qid=Q52824|lt=Jomon period|1=Jōmon period}}, but it is believed to have taken its current form as a shrine during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. Initially, it was dedicated to the {{ill|Kasuga deity|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}}, but {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} enshrined {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|qid=Q261637|lt=Hachiman|1=Hachiman}} as the primary deity after praying for victory in the Oshu Expedition. A natural treasure, the "Matsu of Igusa Hachimangu" (a pine tree planted by Minamoto no Yoritomo), stood at around 40 meters tall but withered in 1973. A portion of its roots is displayed in the shrine corridor. The shrine also served as a model for the North White Snake Shrine in the anime {{ill|Monogatari (series)|en|Monogatari (series)|qid=Q4773554|lt=Monogatari (series)|1=Monogatari (series)}}. == deity == * {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神}} ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ojin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) == History == The site has been inhabited since the Jomon period. The deity was initially {{ill|Kasuga deity|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} until Minamoto no Yoritomo enshrined {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|qid=Q261637|lt=Hachiman|1=Hachiman}} for his military victory during the Oshu Expedition. Later, the Kasuga deity was moved to a subsidiary shrine. In 1477, {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|en|Ōta Dōkan|qid=Q386144|lt=Ota Dokan|1=Ōta Dōkan}} prayed for victory before his siege on Ishigakijo Castle. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the third shogun, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|en|Tokugawa Iemitsu|qid=Q314481|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu|1=Tokugawa Iemitsu}}, commissioned the construction of shrine buildings and granted land as offerings. Subsequent shoguns followed this tradition, resulting in contributions like stone lanterns, torii gates, komainu, and ablution basins. The shrine was expanded and renovated by its parishioners and worshippers during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}. == Grounds == The grounds include a cultural hall, a folk museum, and a kindergarten. == Cultural Properties == * Jomon Period artifact: Face-handled pottery from Igusa Village, now designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. == Parish Area == {{columns-list|2| * Zenpukuji 1–4 chome * Kami-Igusa 1–4 chome * Imagawa 1–4 chome * Momoi 1–4 chome * Shimizu 1 (partial), 2–3 chome * Igusa 1–5 chome * Shimo-Igusa 1 (partial), 2–5 chome * Kami-Ogi 4 chome (partial) * Nishi-Ogikita 3–4 chome, 5 chome (partial) * Hon-Tenuma 2–3 chome (partial) }} == Access == * {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|qid=Q1078110|lt=Chuo Line (Rapid)|1=Chūō Main Line}} {{ill|Nishi-Ogikubo Station|en|Nishi-Ogikubo Station|qid=Q801263|lt=Nishi-Ogikubo Station|1=Nishi-Ogikubo Station}}: 18-minute walk * {{ill|Seibu Shinjuku Line|en|Seibu Shinjuku Line|qid=Q195685|lt=Seibu Shinjuku Line|1=Seibu Shinjuku Line}} {{ill|Kami-Igusa Station|en|Kami-Igusa Station|qid=Q5973346|lt=Kami-Igusa Station|1=Kami-Igusa Station}}: 21-minute walk * {{ill|Kanto Bus|ja|関東バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanto Bus|1=Kanto Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11655856}}: Stops at Igusa Hachimangu or Momoi Fourth Elementary School nearby. == See Also == * [[Hirahama Hachimangu]] * {{ill|Inbe Shrine|en|Inbe Shrine|qid=Q11490722|lt=Inbe Shrine|1=Inbe Shrine}} * [[Hyōgo Kobe Gokoku Shrine]] * [[Nagano Gokoku Shrine]] * [[Kosou Hachimangu]] * [[Tashima Shrine (Saga)|Tashima Shrine]] * [[Ōyama Afuri Shrine|Oyama Aburi Shrine]] == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.igusahachimangu.jp/ Official website (Japanese)] {{List of shrines in Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tokyo]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoritomo]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Igusa Hachimangu Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Suginami, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
